name: MCP Proxy Project
observations: Python tool for switching between MCP (Model Context Protocol) server transports
Provides bidirectional proxying between stdio and SSE/StreamableHTTP transports
Version 0.8.0, supports Python 3.10-3.13
Built on mcp library with uvicorn for HTTP serving
MIT licensed with full type checking using mypy strict mode
Uses UV as package manager with comprehensive CI/CD pipeline
Created comprehensive .agent.md documentation with development guidelines
Implemented flexible Docker solution for automatic MCP package installation
Supports two main modes: stdio to SSE/HTTP and SSE/HTTP to stdio
Entry points: mcp-proxy (main) and mcp-reverse-proxy (client)
Uses PowerShell as default terminal environment
Supports named server configurations and environment variable inheritance

name: Docker Package Installation System
observations: Automatically installs MCP packages during Docker build based on servers.json
Supports both uvx (Python) and npx (Node.js) package managers
Pre-installs packages in Docker image for faster startup and offline capability
No code changes required when adding new MCP servers
Implemented via install_mcp_packages.py script during build stage
Uses multi-stage Docker build with proper caching and security
Solves the problem of manual package management in Docker containers
Eliminates runtime package downloads for faster container startup
Provides offline capability by pre-installing all required packages
Automatically detects package requirements from servers.json changes
Supports both Python (uvx) and Node.js (npx) package ecosystems
Includes comprehensive error handling and logging during build process

name: install_mcp_packages.py
observations: Python script that parses servers.json configuration file
Extracts uvx and npx package names from enabled servers
Installs packages using 'uv tool install' and 'npm install -g'
Handles disabled servers (enabled: false or disabled: true)
Provides graceful error handling and continues with other packages
Supports duplicate removal and comprehensive logging
Main function accepts config file path as command line argument
extract_packages function returns tuple of uvx_packages and npx_packages lists
install_uvx_packages uses subprocess to run 'uv tool install'
install_npx_packages uses subprocess to run 'npm install -g'
Skips packages with empty command or args arrays
Filters out npx flags (starting with -) to get actual package names

name: servers.json Configuration
observations: Defines MCP server configurations with command and args
Currently includes fetch (uvx mcp-server-fetch), time (uvx mcp-server-time), and playwright (npx @playwright/mcp@latest)
Supports enabled/disabled flags for server control
Used by Docker build system to determine packages to install
Flexible format allows adding new servers without code changes
Follows mcpServers structure with transportType stdio
fetch server uses uvx mcp-server-fetch with disabled: false flag
time server uses uvx mcp-server-time without explicit enabled flag
playwright server uses npx @playwright/mcp@latest
All servers use transportType: stdio
Supports timeout configuration (60 seconds for fetch server)
Can be extended with new servers without code changes

name: MCP Protocol
observations: Model Context Protocol for AI assistant integrations
Supports tools, prompts, resources, sampling, and discovery features
Used by various clients like Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, etc.
Enables AI assistants to connect to external data sources and tools
Supports both stdio and SSE/StreamableHTTP transports
Has extensive ecosystem of servers for different functionalities

name: Docker Multi-stage Build
observations: Uses ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.12-alpine as build stage
Installs Node.js/npm during build for npx package support
Runs install_mcp_packages.py to pre-install MCP packages
Copies pre-installed packages to final python:3.12-alpine image
Configures PATH to include uvx tools directory
Runs as non-root user (app:app) with proper security measures
Build stage copies install_mcp_packages.py and servers.json early
Uses mount caches for /root/.cache/uv and /root/.npm for faster builds
Copies /root/.local (uvx tools) and /usr/local/lib/node_modules to final image
Updates PATH to include /home/app/.local/bin for uvx package access
Final image includes wget for health checks at /status endpoint
Maintains security by running as app user with UID/GID 1000

name: Project Structure
observations: Source code in src/mcp_proxy/ with __main__.py entry point
Core modules: mcp_server.py, proxy_server.py, sse_client.py, streamablehttp_client.py
Configuration handling via config_loader.py
Comprehensive test suite in tests/ with pytest and asyncio
Docker deployment with Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and documentation
CI/CD via GitHub Actions with lint, test, coverage, and container publishing
Created supporting documentation: DOCKER_PACKAGE_INSTALLATION.md and SOLUTION_SUMMARY.md
Added test scripts: test_package_installation.py and validate_solution.py
Updated existing .agent.md with new Docker functionality
Maintains separation between source code, tests, configuration, and documentation
Docker-related files include Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and servers.json
CI/CD configuration in .github/workflows/ for automated testing and deployment

name: Development Standards
observations: Strict type checking with mypy in strict mode
Linting with ruff using ALL rules (some exceptions)
100 character line length with Google-style docstrings
Async/await for all I/O operations with proper error handling
83%+ test coverage target using pytest with asyncio support
Pre-commit hooks and comprehensive CI pipeline
